title: Xeraea microcephala
taxonomy: Xeraea microcephala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the genus Xeraea, which comprises a limited number of species. The classification of Xeraea microcephala is further categorized into the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Xeraea microcephala, also known as X. microcephala, is a plant species within the family Gentianaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of X. microcephala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, X. microcephala is often prepared as a tea or tincture, with the most common preparations including a decoction to treat digestive issues and a topical application to soothe skin irritations.
